report_name: Microsoft Scrambles to Patch 'RoguePlanet' Zero-Day in Defender Granting Full System Control

threat_actors.name: Chaotic Eclipse
threat_actors.description: Security researcher who discovered and publicly released the 'RoguePlanet' (CVE-2026-50656) proof-of-concept exploit for Microsoft Defender, citing disputes with Microsoft's bug bounty program.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
attack_patterns.description: The 'RoguePlanet' exploit leverages a race condition and symlink vulnerability (CVE-2026-50656) in the Microsoft Malware Protection Engine to escalate privileges from a local user to NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-50656
vulnerabilities.description: A high-severity local privilege escalation (LPE) zero-day vulnerability in the Microsoft Malware Protection Engine (mpengine.dll). The flaw is a race condition involving a symbolic link weakness that allows a local attacker with low privileges to trick the Defender process into executing code with SYSTEM privileges, even if real-time protection is disabled.

indicators.name: Suspicious Child Process of Microsoft Defender Engine
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Child Process of Microsoft Defender Engine
id: 2b9c1d8e-4f5a-4b6c-8d9e-1a2b3c4d5e6f
status: experimental
description: Detects the Microsoft Defender Antimalware Service Executable (MsMpEng.exe) spawning a command-line interpreter, which could be indicative of exploitation of vulnerabilities like RoguePlanet (CVE-2026-50656).
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/microsoft/microsoft-working-on-defender-patch-for-rogueplanet-zero-day/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-19
tags:
  - attack.t1068
  - attack.privilege_escalation
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\MsMpEng.exe'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate but rare actions by Defender for remediation scripts.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects when the MsMpEng.exe process spawns suspicious child processes, such as cmd.exe or powershell.exe, with elevated privileges, which may indicate exploitation of CVE-2026-50656.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Child Process of Microsoft Defender Engine
